title: Generative AI at Work
abstract: This paper presents evidence from a randomized controlled trial examining how access to generative AI suggestions affects productivity of workers in customer support. We find substantial productivity gains.
key_findings: 14% productivity increase for customer support agents
Larger gains for less experienced workers

title: The Effects of Generative AI on High-Skilled Work: Evidence from Three Field Experiments with Software Developers
abstract: We conduct three randomized controlled trials with software developers at Microsoft, Accenture, and a Fortune 100 company to examine the impact of generative AI coding tools on developer productivity.
key_findings: 21% time reduction on tasks with AI
Larger gains for less experienced developers

title: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity
abstract: Randomized controlled trial examining how AI tools affect the productivity of experienced open-source developers working on their own repositories.
key_findings: AI provides different value for exploration vs execution

title: The Projected Impact of Generative AI on Future Productivity Growth
abstract: Analysis of generative AI's impact on aggregate productivity growth and total factor productivity (TFP) across firms.
key_findings: Current TFP impact: 0.01 percentage points in 2025
Adoption rates critical to realized gains

title: AI, Productivity, and Labor Markets: A Review of the Empirical Evidence
abstract: Comprehensive review of empirical evidence on AI's impact on productivity, employment, and labor markets.
key_findings: Distribution of gains varies by worker skill

title: Generative AI, Productivity and the Future of Work
authors: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis
abstract: Analysis of adoption trends and productivity impacts of generative AI across different worker categories and organizational contexts.
key_findings: 2.2 hours per week productivity gain average
Adoption increasing from 26.4% to 33.7% of workers
